title: Preserve query snapshots across pages
description: Bind paginated actuals to the transaction membership, evidence and Reconciliation State, attribution, and exact grouped totals established by the first page. Implements G6 and UC-LEDGER-005 under C15.
rationale: An agent must not reconcile pages drawn from different Ledger states.
story_want: page through a stable actuals result
acceptance_criteria: Given a filtered actuals query requiring multiple pages, when the first page is requested, then the result includes a snapshot-bound opaque cursor, deterministic ordering, evidence and Reconciliation State, current category, pool, instrument and Cardholder Attribution, and totals for the full selected set.

Given a valid cursor, when a later page is requested after unrelated Ledger mutations, then membership, state, attribution, and pool, category, and pool-by-category totals remain those of the first-page snapshot.

Given a cursor with altered filters, invalid encoding, unsupported version, or expired snapshot, when a page is requested, then a stable cursor error is returned and no partial page or live-state fallback is exposed.

Given all pages from one snapshot, when their stable transaction identifiers are combined, then each selected Canonical Transaction appears exactly once and page aggregates reconcile to the first-page full-set totals.
